description: Bubble Tea alt-screen TUI: one full-width merged stdout/stderr log view (placeholder stream until imux-38oz). acceptance: UI state is driven by central model, not ad-hoc globals
notes: Shipped: internal/tui (model.go, overlay.go) + cli tui -> tui.Run(). Layout: single log surface; p/i/? open overlays; Esc closes (help returns prior overlay). description: Implement per-process controls: pause, continue, kill, and restart, with consistent state transitions and user-visible outcomes.

title: Support dynamic process creation from UI
TUI: multirun-style colored prefixes and merged process output
description:
MVP backlog covered stream tagging (imux-38oz) but not the main usability win from gastrolog multirun: a single merged view where every line is immediately attributable and scannable via color + bracketed process name.

Reference implementation: gastrolog backend/cmd/multirun/main.go — fixed ANSI palette cycled by child index, lineWriter mutex so concurrent scanners cannot interleave partial lines, bufio.Scanner line boundaries, writeTo(prefix,color,line) formatting, optional tee without ANSI.

Current imux: EventProcessOutput is appended as plain text in internal/tui/model.go (drainEvents / appendLogLine); composeLines still pads with placeholder stream lines when the buffer is short. There is no per-process lipgloss/ANSI styling in the merged surface, so dense multi-process output is harder to read than multirun.

Scope: extend the TUI merged log (and align with imux run output where practical) so rendered lines match multirun ergonomics—color assignment keyed by process id/name, reset-safe strings, and preserved ordering. Document any Bubble Tea / lipgloss constraints (e.g. width-aware truncation with styles). Consider whether child process ANSI should pass through, be stripped, or be nested behind the prefix policy.
acceptance: Merged log uses distinct stable colors per process (like gastrolog multirun), prefixes remain readable on dark terminals, stdout vs stderr visually distinguishable, and long/interleaved bursts do not corrupt lines or lose serial ordering guarantees.
notes: External ref: ~/Code/gastrolog/backend/cmd/multirun/main.go (lineWriter, colors, scanLines, startChild pipe wiring).

title: TUI: multirun-style colored prefixes and merged process output
TUI: multirun-style colored prefixes and merged process output
description:
MVP backlog covered stream tagging (imux-38oz) but not the main usability win from gastrolog multirun: a single merged view where every line is immediately attributable and scannable via color + bracketed process name.

Reference implementation: gastrolog backend/cmd/multirun/main.go — fixed ANSI palette cycled by child index, lineWriter mutex so concurrent scanners cannot interleave partial lines, bufio.Scanner line boundaries, writeTo(prefix,color,line) formatting, optional tee without ANSI.

Current imux: EventProcessOutput is appended as plain text in internal/tui/model.go (drainEvents / appendLogLine); composeLines still pads with placeholder stream lines when the buffer is short. There is no per-process lipgloss/ANSI styling in the merged surface, so dense multi-process output is harder to read than multirun.

## Storage and memory

- Do not hold the full merged log in RAM. Persist session lines to a file-backed store.
- Use an unlinked temp file (open temp, then unlink path while keeping the fd) so the log does not clutter the filesystem and is reclaimed when the process exits.
- Add a `--tee` flag (TUI entrypoint, analogous to `imux run --tee`) for an optional persisted path that receives plain, non-ANSI merged lines.

## TUI log UX

- Togglable visibility for stdout vs stderr (and clear UI affordance for current state).
- Togglable timestamps on displayed lines.
- Scroll the log view: Page Up / Page Down and mouse scroll wheel (enable appropriate Bubble Tea mouse mode).
- Filter the log: support both glob and regex (document how the user selects mode, e.g. prefix `glob:` / `re:` or separate flags).

Scope: extend the TUI merged log (and align with `imux run` output where practical) so rendered lines match multirun ergonomics—color assignment keyed by process id/name, reset-safe strings, and preserved ordering. Document any Bubble Tea / lipgloss constraints (e.g. width-aware truncation with styles). Consider whether child process ANSI should pass through, be stripped, or be nested behind the prefix policy.
acceptance: Merged log uses distinct stable colors per process (like gastrolog multirun), prefixes remain readable on dark terminals, stdout vs stderr visually distinguishable, and long/interleaved bursts do not corrupt lines or lose serial ordering guarantees. Log content is not kept in an unbounded in-memory slice: primary retention is the unlinked temp backing file. `--tee` writes plain non-ANSI lines to the given path. TUI supports toggling stdout/stderr and timestamps, scrolling via PgUp/PgDn and wheel, and filtering (glob and regex).

- Scroll the log view: Page Up / Page Down and mouse scroll wheel (enable appropriate Bubble Tea mouse mode).
- Filter the log: support both glob and regex (document how the user selects mode, e.g. prefix `glob:` / `re:` or separate flags).

Scope: extend the TUI merged log (and align with `imux run` output where practical) so rendered lines match multirun ergonomics—color assignment keyed by process id/name, reset-safe strings, and preserved ordering. Document any Bubble Tea / lipgloss constraints (e.g. width-aware truncation with styles). Consider whether child process ANSI should pass through, be stripped, or be nested behind the prefix policy.
acceptance: Merged log uses distinct stable colors per process (like gastrolog multirun), prefixes remain readable on dark terminals, stdout vs stderr visually distinguishable, and long/interleaved bursts do not corrupt lines or lose serial ordering guarantees. Log content is not kept in an unbounded in-memory slice: primary retention is the unlinked temp backing file. `--tee` writes plain non-ANSI lines to the given path. TUI supports toggling stdout/stderr and timestamps, scrolling via PgUp/PgDn and wheel, and filtering (glob and regex). The log is a single unified multiplexed timeline: changing dock focus does not swap or re-scope the log to the selected process only.
notes: Implemented: internal/sessionlog (unlinked JSONL + int64 offset sidecar, optional --tee plain RFC3339 lines). TUI: disk-backed log (no in-memory event slice), per-process colored [k|name] prefixes (lipgloss palette), O/E/@ toggles stdout/stderr/timestamps, PgUp/PgDn + wheel scroll over log, / filter overlay (Tab glob|regex, Enter apply), g tail. imux tui --tee and --log-filter (glob:/re:/bare glob). Removed selection-dependent placeholder stream; neutral empty state. Help/footer updated. Branch task/imux-1zcu-unified-log. go test ./... go vet ./...
close_reason: Unified merged log on disk, per-process styling, filters/signals/help/footer polish; acceptance met.

CI: GitHub Actions release binaries + Homebrew formula
description:
Add GitHub Actions workflows to build and publish imux release artifacts and to keep a Homebrew tap formula in sync.

## Inspiration (copy patterns, adapt paths)

- **gqlt**: https://github.com/kluzzebass/gqlt/blob/main/.github/workflows/release.yml — tag-triggered workflow (`v*`), `go test`, multi-GOOS/GOARCH static builds into `dist/`, `sha256sum`, `softprops/action-gh-release`, then checkout `kluzzebass/homebrew-tap` with `HOMEBREW_TAP_TOKEN`, generate `*.rb` from checksums, commit + push.
- **reflag**: https://github.com/kluzzebass/reflag/blob/main/.github/workflows/release.yml — same overall shape as gqlt (paired `ci.yml` + `release.yml`).

## imux-specific notes

- Entrypoint is the **module root** (`go build -o … .`) not `./cmd/…`; adjust artifact names (e.g. `imux-linux-amd64`) and formula `install`/`test` accordingly.
- Consider adding **version/commit/date linker flags** (gqlt sets `-X main.version=…`) if the CLI should print a meaningful `--version`; wire flags in `internal/cli` as needed.
- **Secrets**: document required repo secret `HOMEBREW_TAP_TOKEN` (fine-grained PAT with push to homebrew-tap) if using the same tap-update approach.
- **CI workflow**: mirror gqlt/reflag `ci.yml` (Go test on push/PR) if not already present.

## Deliverables

1. `.github/workflows/ci.yml` (or equivalent) — test on PR/push.
2. `.github/workflows/release.yml` — on `v*` tag: test, build matrix of binaries, GitHub Release + checksums, optional Homebrew tap PR/commit.

Document in README or docs how to cut a release (tag naming, tap install).
acceptance: - Pushing a semver tag `v*` produces a GitHub Release with per-platform imux binaries + `checksums.txt` (or equivalent).\n- Homebrew install path documented (`brew install …/imux` from kluzzebass/homebrew-tap or chosen tap); formula updates automatically on release or clear manual steps if tap update is deferred.\n- CI runs `go test ./...` on pull requests and mainline pushes.
notes: Refs: gqlt release.yml (homebrew-tap checkout + embedded formula), reflag same layout.

TUI: process list and command bar disappear in tmux on resize/scroll
description:
When imux runs inside tmux, the process list and command bar sometimes disappear from the screen, especially after the tmux window is resized or the tmux scrollback buffer is scrolled up. The TUI does not appear to redraw itself.

Workarounds the user found:
- Cycling processes with Tab forces a repaint
- Toggling the help modal on/off forces a repaint

Likely cause: missing full-screen redraw on terminal size events, or the alt-screen/scroll interaction with tmux is not handled. Bubbletea apps usually need to handle tea.WindowSizeMsg and re-render, and may need a periodic ClearScreen / full repaint trigger.

Acceptance criteria:
- Reproduce the disappearance inside tmux (resize the pane, scroll up, scroll back to bottom)
- Identify why the process list / command bar are not repainted
- Fix so that the TUI repaints reliably without user input after resize and after exiting tmux scrollback
- Verify in tmux on macOS

TUI: enable mouse wheel scroll (logs + dock)
description:
Enable mouse support so users can scroll the log pane and the dock with the wheel, including when running under tmux.

Current state:
- internal/tui/model.go:2799 starts the program with only tea.WithAltScreen(). Mouse events are not requested, so tea.MouseMsg never reaches Update.
- Inside tmux without 'set -g mouse on', tmux intercepts the wheel into copy-mode. With 'mouse on' enabled, wheel events forward to the alt-screen app as SGR mouse sequences.

Implementation sketch:
- Add tea.WithMouseCellMotion() to the ProgramOption list (cell motion is enough for wheel + click; WithMouseAllMotion is overkill and noisy).
- Handle tea.MouseMsg in Update:
  - tea.MouseWheelUp / MouseWheelDown over the log pane -> adjust m.logScroll (same direction semantics as PgUp/PgDown but smaller step, e.g. 3 lines).
  - Wheel over the dock area -> adjust m.dockScroll.
  - Decide pane based on Y coordinate vs layoutHeights().
- Optional follow-up: click on a dock entry to select it, click on a log line to copy, etc. Out of scope for this ticket — stick to scroll first.

User-facing notes:
- Document that tmux users need 'set -g mouse on' for the wheel to reach imux.
- Holding Shift bypasses application mouse handling and gives the user back native terminal selection / their terminal's scrollback — keep this behavior intact (don't enable WithMouseAllMotion which can interfere with selection).

Acceptance criteria:
- Mouse wheel over the log pane scrolls log content up/down.
- Mouse wheel over the dock scrolls the dock list when it overflows.
- Works inside tmux (with 'mouse on') and in a bare terminal.
- Shift+drag still allows native terminal selection.
